selected_important_turn.text: Alright... my baby is on formula and needs water for her bottles. My son snacks all day and really needs food. Otherwise, he will starve and won't thrive
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn shifts the interaction from the opponent's cooperative information-sharing into a strongly self-focused, emotional claim. It frames the speaker's needs as urgent and attempts to increase their own share rather than opening trade or proposing a workable split, so it materially shapes bargaining stakes and framing.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: As phrased, it's purely an appeal: it increases claim without proposing how the other party's needs will be met or creating trades. That risks locking the negotiation into a zero-sum moral-claim contest rather than enabling mutually beneficial tradeoffs. Because the other party had signaled cooperation, this was a missed chance to translate shared needs into a concrete, fair split.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Keep the personal details (to signal need) but pair them immediately with a collaborative, integrative move: ask the other party about their priorities, propose a split or trade that addresses both households (e.g., suggest splitting water/food and offering firewood in exchange), or invite brainstorming about feasible divisions. Soften purely urgent language and add a cooperative proposal or question.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — by opening trade options and enabling integrative splits rather than pure claims
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — by making explicit concern for balancing both sides' core needs
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — clearer, collaborative proposals reduce friction and speed agreement
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — moving from pleading to problem-solving builds trust and reduces resentment

value2reason.Low: I will bring two coats along on the trip.
value2reason.Medium: I am a somewhat thirsty person.

value2reason.Low: extra water needed for hydration and cleaning hands
value2reason.Medium: extra food needed in case my basic supply runs out or food gets spoiled
value2reason.High: extra firewood needed for warmth and cooking food

dialogue.text: Hello! I am a mother of two and I have two kids who need extra food and water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Okay, I need food and water too unfortunately. We can discuss this.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Alright... my baby is on formula and needs water for her bottles. My son snacks all day and really needs food. Otherwise, he will starve and won't thrive
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I really need more firewood for warmth and cooking. But I’m willing to split the food and water if you can take some of the firewood. What do you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand the need for firewood, but I’m more focused on food since it’s my highest priority. How about we split the food 2-1, and I take 2 firewood packages while you take 1? That way, I get more of what I need, and you still get some warmth.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate your focus on food, but firewood is critical for warmth and cooking. How about we split the food 2-1 and share the firewood 2-1? That way, both of us get what we need without one side feeling shortchanged.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I’m okay with splitting food 2-1, but I’d prefer to take 2 firewood packages for warmth. Let’s agree on 2 food, 2 firewood for me, and 1 food, 1 firewood for you. That way, we both get what we need without conflict.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think splitting food 2-1 and firewood 2-1 is fair. It meets both our needs without leaving anyone out. Let’s confirm this split: 2 food, 2 firewood for you, and 1 food, 1 firewood for me.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to this split. 2 food and 2 firewood for me, 1 food and 1 firewood for you. Let’s wrap this up and head out.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I agree to this split. 2 food and 2 firewood for you, 1 food and 1 firewood for me. Let’s wrap this up and head out.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Agreed on the split. Let's head out.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
